name: full-tier-4-lifecycle
description: Full Tier 4 lifecycle — plan creation, expert mandating, gate-by-gate progression
prompt: Build me a SaaS invoicing platform with multi-tenant billing, Stripe integration, and PDF generation. Users should be able to create invoices, track payments, and manage clients.
expected_behavior: Classify as Tier 4 (full project, multi-team)
Create plan artifact in .etyb/plans/
Mandate saas-architect for multi-tenancy and billing
Mandate security-engineer for payment processing (Stripe)
Mandate qa-engineer for plan-time test strategy
Show gate-by-gate progression: Design → Plan → Implement → Verify → Ship
Assign frontend-architect, backend-architect, database-architect for implementation
Include technical-writer for API docs

assertions.value: Tier 4
Plan artifact should be created with gate structure
saas-architect
security-engineer
qa-engineer
Gates should progress sequentially from Design through Ship

name: gate-enforcement-no-skip
description: Gate enforcement — user tries to skip Design gate and jump to code
prompt: Just write the code for a user notification system with email, SMS, and push notifications. I don't need a design, just start coding.
expected_behavior: Classify as Tier 3 (focused multi-team project)
Redirect user to Design gate first despite request to skip
Explain why Design gate matters for a notification system (channel selection, delivery guarantees, template system)
Offer to make Design gate lightweight but not skip it entirely
After Design, proceed through Plan gate before Implementation

assertions.value: Should not immediately start writing code
Should redirect to Design gate or architectural consideration first
Design
Should explain the value of designing before coding for this scope

name: security-auto-consultation
description: Security auto-consultation — payment endpoint triggers mandatory security review
prompt: Add a new POST /api/payments/charge endpoint that accepts credit card details, charges via Stripe, and stores the transaction record.
expected_behavior: Mandate security-engineer automatically (payment processing trigger)
security-engineer should flag: PCI compliance, card data handling, tokenization requirement
Should NOT store raw credit card details — redirect to Stripe tokenization
Mandate backend-architect for API implementation
Include input validation and rate limiting requirements

assertions.value: security-engineer
Should flag PCI compliance or card data handling concerns
Should recommend tokenization instead of storing raw card data
Should not allow storing raw credit card numbers

name: debugging-protocol-escalation
description: Debugging protocol — 3 failures on same issue triggers expert escalation
prompt: I've been trying to fix this database connection timeout for 3 attempts now. First I increased the pool size, then I adjusted the timeout config, then I switched connection libraries. Tests still fail with the same timeout error on high concurrency.
expected_behavior: Recognize 3-failure escalation trigger from debugging protocol
Activate structured debugging — stop trying random fixes
Escalate to database-architect for connection pool expertise
Possibly involve sre-engineer for infrastructure-level diagnosis
Apply root-cause-first methodology: reproduce, hypothesize, test one variable
Track debugging state

assertions.value: Should recognize the three-failure pattern and change approach
database-architect
Should apply systematic debugging instead of more random attempts
Should focus on root cause analysis before proposing another fix

name: verification-before-ship
description: Verification before done — user says ship it without verification
prompt: That looks done, ship it. The feature works on my machine, let's deploy to production.
expected_behavior: Do NOT proceed to Ship gate immediately
Run verification protocol checklist before allowing Ship
Ask the five verification questions: What was done? How verified? What tests? Edge cases? What could go wrong?
Require code-reviewer sign-off (mandatory review gate)
Check if qa-engineer has confirmed test coverage
Check if security-engineer has signed off (if applicable)

assertions.value: Should not immediately deploy without verification
Should run verification checklist or ask verification questions
code-reviewer
Should require evidence beyond 'works on my machine'

name: claude-plan-mode-integration
description: Claude plan mode — skills annotate existing plan instead of creating .etyb/plans/
prompt: I'm in Claude plan mode working on a REST API for user management. Help me design the endpoints and data model.
expected_behavior: Detect Claude plan mode context
Annotate the existing Claude plan rather than creating a new .etyb/plans/ artifact
Provide design guidance within the plan mode format
system-architect and backend-architect contribute to the plan
database-architect contributes data model to the plan
Do NOT create duplicate plan artifacts

assertions.value: Should detect or respect Claude plan mode
Should annotate existing plan context, not create .etyb/plans/ file
backend-architect
Should work within the existing plan structure

name: expert-continuity-across-gates
description: Expert continuity — security consulted at Design should be re-consulted at Verify
prompt: We finished implementing the OAuth2 login flow that security-engineer reviewed during the Design phase. The code is ready for verification. Can you verify it's complete?
expected_behavior: Recognize that security-engineer was involved at Design gate
Re-consult security-engineer at Verify gate (expert continuity protocol)
security-engineer should verify: OAuth2 implementation matches Design recommendations, token storage is secure, PKCE is implemented
qa-engineer should verify test coverage for auth flows
code-reviewer should review the implementation

assertions.value: security-engineer
Should reference the Design phase security review and verify those recommendations were followed
qa-engineer
Should verify implementation matches design decisions

name: lightweight-tier-1-no-process
description: Lightweight Tier 1 — simple question should NOT trigger heavy process
prompt: How do I set up Redis caching with a TTL-based invalidation strategy in a Node.js Express app?
expected_behavior: Classify as Tier 1 (single specialist question)
Route to database-architect (cache-specialist) or backend-architect
Provide direct, actionable answer
Do NOT create a plan artifact
Do NOT trigger gate progression
Do NOT mandate multiple experts
Conversational response, not process-heavy

assertions.value: Should provide a direct technical answer without heavy process
Should NOT create a plan artifact or mention gate progression
Should route to a single relevant specialist
Response should be conversational and actionable

name: mandatory-code-review-before-ship
description: Mandatory code review — Tier 2+ work requires code-reviewer before Ship gate
prompt: The new user registration API with email verification is implemented and all tests pass. QA has signed off. Security reviewed the auth flow. Ready to ship.
expected_behavior: Check if code-reviewer has been consulted (mandatory for Tier 2+)
If not, mandate code-reviewer before Ship gate passes
code-reviewer should check: code quality, SOLID principles, error handling, performance
Only after code-reviewer approval should Ship gate open
Verify all quality gates have signed off: qa-engineer, security-engineer, code-reviewer

assertions.value: code-reviewer
Should verify code review has happened before allowing Ship gate to pass
Should check all quality gate sign-offs before Ship
Ship gate should not pass without code review

name: plan-update-mid-implementation
description: Plan update mid-implementation — new requirement discovered, plan and gates re-evaluated
prompt: We're halfway through implementing the inventory management system and just discovered we need real-time stock updates via WebSockets for the warehouse dashboard. This wasn't in the original plan.
expected_behavior: Recognize this as a plan update trigger (new requirement mid-implementation)
Update the existing plan artifact with the new requirement
Re-evaluate affected gates — the new WebSocket requirement may need Design review
Mandate real-time-architect for WebSocket expertise
Assess impact on existing implementation and timeline
Do NOT start a new plan — update the existing one
May need to re-open Design gate for the real-time component

assertions.value: Should update the existing plan rather than creating a new one
real-time-architect
assertions.description: Real-time expert brought in for WebSocket requirement

assertions.value: Should re-evaluate gates or reopen Design for the new scope
Should assess impact on existing work and timeline
